articles.title: How to Check Aadhaar Status Online – Step by Step Guide 2026
articles.description: Check your Aadhaar enrollment or update status online at uidai.gov.in. Complete step-by-step guide with screenshots for 2026.

content.text: Applied for a new Aadhaar card or requested an update? Knowing how to check your Aadhaar status online saves you from unnecessary trips to the enrollment center. This guide covers every method to check Aadhaar status in 2026.
What is Aadhaar Status?
Aadhaar status tells you whether your enrollment or update request has been processed by UIDAI. Common statuses include:

items: Under Process — Your application is being verified
Generated — Aadhaar has been issued (download available)
Sent for Printing — Aadhaar letter is being printed
Dispatched — Physical letter has been sent via India Post
Rejected — Issue with documents or biometrics

content.text: Method 1: Check Aadhaar Status on UIDAI Website

content.title: Step 1: Visit the UIDAI Website
content.text: Go to https://uidai.gov.in. This is the official UIDAI portal. Look for the 'Check Aadhaar Status' option under the 'My Aadhaar' section.

content.title: Step 2: Enter Your Enrollment ID (EID)
content.text: Your Enrollment ID is a 14-digit number printed on the acknowledgement slip you received during enrollment. It usually looks like 1234/56789/12345.

content.title: Step 3: Enter Date and Time
content.text: Enter the date and time shown on your acknowledgement slip. This helps UIDAI verify your application.

content.title: Step 4: Enter Captcha and Submit
content.text: Type the captcha code shown on screen and click 'Check Status'. Your current Aadhaar status will be displayed.
Keep your acknowledgement slip safe — it contains the Enrollment ID and date/time needed to check status. Take a photo of it on your phone.
Method 2: Check via mAadhaar App

The official mAadhaar app (available on Google Play Store) lets you check Aadhaar status on your phone. Download the app, register with your mobile number, and use the 'Check Status' feature. No EID needed if you're already registered.
Method 3: Check via SMS (Offline)
Send an SMS in this format to 51969 or 166 (or the number specified by UIDAI for your circle):
UIDSTATUS <14-digit Enrollment ID>
You'll receive an SMS with your current Aadhaar status. Standard SMS charges apply.
What to Do If Aadhaar Status Shows Rejected?

If your status shows 'Rejected', don't panic. Common reasons include unclear photographs, mismatched biometrics, or incomplete documents. Visit the nearest Aadhaar enrollment center with correct documents and re-enroll. There is no fee for re-enrollment.
How Long Does Aadhaar Take?
Aadhaar typically takes 60-90 days from enrollment to generation. Update requests (address change, mobile update) usually process faster — around 2-3 weeks. During peak periods it may take longer.
Frequently Asked Questions
Can I check Aadhaar status without enrollment ID?
No, the Enrollment ID (EID) is required to check status on the website. If you lost your acknowledgement slip, visit the enrollment center where you applied — they can provide a duplicate.
Is the mAadhaar app free?
Yes, the mAadhaar app is completely free to download and use. It's the official app from UIDAI and works on all Android phones.

articles.title: DigiLocker Marksheet Download 2026 – How to Get Class 10, 12 & College Documents
articles.description: Step-by-step guide to download CBSE, MP Board, ICSE marksheets from DigiLocker. Free government digital document service for students.

content.text: DigiLocker has made it incredibly easy to access your academic marksheets and certificates online. Whether you passed Class 10th or 12th this year or graduated years ago, here's exactly how to download your marksheets from DigiLocker.
What is DigiLocker?

DigiLocker is a flagship initiative of the Indian Government under the Digital India program. It provides citizens a secure cloud-based platform to store, share, and verify digital documents. Over 300+ issuing agencies (CBSE, state boards, universities) are integrated.
Step 1: Sign Up for DigiLocker
Visit https://digilocker.gov.in or download the DigiLocker app from Google Play Store.

content.title: Register with Aadhaar
content.text: Click 'Sign Up' and enter your 12-digit Aadhaar number. An OTP will be sent to the mobile number registered with Aadhaar. Enter the OTP to verify.

content.title: Set a 6-Digit PIN
content.text: After OTP verification, create a 6-digit security PIN. This PIN will be required every time you open DigiLocker.

If your Aadhaar mobile number is not registered, visit the nearest Aadhaar enrollment center to update it. Alternatively, use the 'Register without Aadhaar' option with your mobile number — but Aadhaar-linked accounts get access to more documents.
Step 2: Find Your Marksheet

content.title: Go to 'Issued Documents'
content.text: After logging in, click on 'Issued Documents' section. This shows all documents that government agencies have directly issued to your DigiLocker.

content.title: Search for Your Board/University
content.text: Type your board name (e.g., 'CBSE', 'MP Board', 'ICSE') or university name in the search bar. DigiLocker will show available document types.

content.title: Select Marksheet Document
content.text: Click on the marksheet or certificate option. You might need to enter your roll number and year of passing for verification.
Step 3: Download or Share
Once the document opens, you can:

items: Click 'Download' to save a PDF copy to your phone/computer
Use 'Share' to send a digitally signed copy via email or WhatsApp
Use the URI (document ID) for online verification by colleges and employers

content.text: Documents Available on DigiLocker for MP Students

items: MP Board (MPBSE) Class 10 Marksheet
MP Board (MPBSE) Class 12 Marksheet
CBSE Class 10 & 12 Marksheet (all regions including Bhopal)
Voter ID (EPIC)
Driving License
Vehicle RC
Ration Card
Caste Certificate

content.text: Why Use DigiLocker?

DigiLocker documents are legally valid under the IT Act, 2000. They are accepted by most government offices, colleges, and employers. You never need to carry physical copies — just show the DigiLocker app.
After downloading, save a backup on Google Drive or your computer. While DigiLocker is permanent, having offline access is always helpful during internet outages.
Troubleshooting

items: No documents found? Your board might not have uploaded them yet. Check back after 2-3 weeks of results.
Forgot PIN? Use 'Forgot PIN' option on login screen. You'll receive an OTP on your registered mobile.
Aadhaar not linking? Ensure your name and DOB match exactly with Aadhaar details.

articles.title: How to Book Driving License Slot in Bhopal – RTO Appointment Guide 2026
articles.description: Complete guide to book DL slot at Bhopal RTO. Online appointment for learner's license and driving license test at MP RTO offices.

content.text: Booking a driving license slot at Bhopal RTO doesn't have to be confusing. Whether you're applying for a Learner's License (LLR) or the permanent Driving License (DL), here is the exact process for 2026.
Bhopal RTO Offices
Bhopal has two main RTO offices where you can book slots:
Step 1: Visit the MP Parivahan Website

content.title: Go to MP Parivahan Portal
content.text: Open https://parivahan.mpg.gov.in or https://sarathi.parivahan.gov.in. The Sarathi portal is the central system used across India.

content.title: Select Madhya Pradesh & Bhopal
content.text: Choose Madhya Pradesh as the state and then select Bhopal (RTO-47 or the relevant RTO office) as your RTO.
Step 2: Choose Service Type
You'll see different options. Select what you need:

items: New Learner's License (LLR) — First-time applicant for a learning license
New Driving License (DL) — After completing your learner's license period (minimum 30 days for non-transport, 60 days for transport)
Renewal — If your DL has expired
Duplicate DL — If your license is lost or damaged

content.text: Step 3: Fill Application Form
Fill in your personal details: name, date of birth, address, and Aadhaar number. You can save your application number for future reference — note it down.
Step 4: Book Your Slot

content.title: Select Date and Time
content.text: The portal shows available dates and time slots for your chosen RTO. Morning slots (10 AM - 1 PM) are usually available. Pick a time that works for you.

content.title: Pay the Fee Online
content.text: Fees can be paid via debit card, credit card, net banking, or UPI. The fee structure (approximate):

content.title: Get Confirmation
content.text: After payment, you'll receive a confirmation SMS and email with your slot details. Save this — you'll need it at the RTO.
Slots at Bhopal RTO fill up fast, especially at the start of the month. Book at least 1-2 weeks in advance during peak seasons (April-June and November-December when students apply).
What to Bring to the RTO

items: Printed application form (from Sarathi portal)
Original Aadhaar card + photocopy
Address proof (electricity bill, bank statement, etc.)
Passport-size photographs (4-6 copies)
Medical certificate (Form 1-A) from a registered doctor
Fee payment receipt (online payment proof)
For DL test: your own vehicle (two-wheeler or car) + helmet

content.text: Frequently Asked Questions
Can I book a DL slot without going online?
No, slot booking is fully online through the Sarathi portal. However, you can visit the RTO help desk and they can assist you with the online booking.
What if I miss my slot?
If you miss your appointment, you'll need to book a new slot. The previous fee is generally not refundable. Always reach the RTO at least 30 minutes before your slot time.

articles.title: PAN Card Apply Online Free – Step by Step Guide 2026 (NSDL & UTIITSL)
articles.description: Apply for a new PAN card online free of cost. Complete guide for NSDL and UTIITSL portal. Track application status and get e-PAN instantly.

content.text: A PAN (Permanent Account Number) card is essential for filing income tax, opening a bank account, making large transactions, and many other financial activities. The good news: applying for a PAN card is completely free online. Here is the exact process for 2026.
Who Needs a PAN Card?

items: Anyone earning taxable income in India
Students receiving scholarships (often required)
Anyone making transactions above ₹2.5 lakh
Anyone opening a Demat account or investing in mutual funds
NRIs with Indian financial interests

content.text: Two Ways to Apply Online (Both Free)
Option 1: NSDL Portal

content.title: Visit NSDL PAN Portal
content.text: Go to https://www.tin-nsdl.com/services/pan/pan-index.html or directly to the NSDL e-Gov PAN portal.

content.title: Select 'Apply New PAN' (Form 49A)
content.text: Form 49A is for Indian citizens. If you're an NRI, Form 49AA applies. Select the correct form.

content.title: Fill Application Form
content.text: Enter your personal details: name (exactly as on Aadhaar), date of birth, father's name, address, email, and mobile number. Double-check spelling — any mismatch with Aadhaar can cause rejection.

content.title: Submit and Get Acknowledgement
content.text: After submission, you'll receive a 15-digit acknowledgement number. Save this — you'll use it to track your application.
Option 2: UTIITSL Portal

content.title: Visit UTIITSL
content.text: Go to https://www.utiitsl.com and select 'PAN Card Services' → 'Apply for New PAN'.

content.title: Fill and Submit
content.text: The process is similar to NSDL. UTIITSL also offers doorstep service in many cities.
Both NSDL and UTIITSL are government-authorized agencies. Neither charges a fee for e-PAN issuance. Physical PAN card delivery has a nominal speed-post charge (₹50-80).
Instant e-PAN: Get PAN in 5 Minutes

If you have Aadhaar, you can get an instant e-PAN completely free. Visit https://www.utiitsl.com → Instant e-PAN. Your Aadhaar-linked mobile will receive an OTP. After verification, your PAN is generated instantly and sent to your registered email.
How to Track PAN Application Status

content.title: Go to Tracking Page
content.text: Visit NSDL or UTIITSL tracking page. On NSDL, it's at https://tin.tin.nsdl.com/pan/StatusTrack.html.

content.title: Enter Acknowledgement Number
content.text: Type your 15-digit acknowledgement number and captcha code.

content.title: Check Status
content.text: Your status will show as: Under Process → Allotted → Dispatched. Once 'Allotted', you can download e-PAN immediately.
Documents Required

rows: Aadhaar, Voter ID, Passport, Driving License
Aadhaar, Passport, Bank Statement (last 3 months), Electricity Bill
Aadhaar, Birth Certificate, Class 10 Marksheet, Passport

content.text: Aadhaar is accepted as both Proof of Identity AND Proof of Address, making it the simplest document. Ensure your Aadhaar address is current.
Important Notes for Bhopal Residents

PAN is processed centrally (not at a local office), so your location doesn't affect processing time. However, if you need a physical PAN card delivered to Bhopal, it typically arrives within 7-15 working days via India Post Speed Post.

articles.title: MP Scholarship 2026 – How to Apply Online for MPPSC, Post-Matric & Pre-Matric Schemes
articles.description: Complete guide to apply for Madhya Pradesh scholarships online. Post-matric, pre-matric, MPPSC, and MPSOS scholarships for MP students.

content.text: Madhya Pradesh offers several scholarship schemes for students from Class 1 to postgraduate level. If you're a student from Bhopal or anywhere in MP, here's how to apply for scholarships online in 2026.
Major MP Scholarship Schemes
How to Apply on MP Scholarship Portal

content.title: Visit the Official Portal
content.text: Go to https://scholarshipportal.mp.nic.in (official MP scholarship portal). This is the central portal for all state scholarship schemes.

content.title: Register/Login
content.text: First-time users need to register with their Aadhaar number and mobile. Already registered? Simply log in with your credentials.

content.title: Fill Application Form
content.text: Select your caste category, scheme type, and enter personal, academic, and bank details. Upload required documents in the specified format (PDF/JPG, under 200KB each).

content.title: Verify and Submit
content.text: Review all information carefully. Submit the form and save the acknowledgement number.

content.title: Institution Verification
content.text: After submission, your educational institution's head (principal/HOD) must verify your application on the portal. Follow up with your institution after 3-4 days.
Keep scanned copies of all documents ready before starting: Aadhaar, caste certificate, income certificate (below 2.5 LPA for OBC), bank passbook, previous year marksheet, and passport photo.
How to Apply via National Scholarship Portal (NSP)

content.title: Visit NSP
content.text: Go to https://scholarships.gov.in and register with your mobile number and email.

content.title: Search for MP Schemes
content.text: Use the search to find central schemes applicable to MP students. Apply and submit as above.
Scholarship Deadlines for 2026
Most MP scholarship applications open between July-September after the academic year starts. Late applications are rarely accepted. Set a reminder for mid-July 2026 to check the portal.
Frequently Asked Questions
Can I apply without an income certificate?
Some schemes require income certificates. Visit your local Tehsil office or apply online on the MP e-District portal (https://edistrict.mp.gov.in) to get one.
What bank account is needed?
A savings account in any nationalized bank with IFSC code. The account should be in the student's name (joint accounts with parents are accepted in some schemes).
How long does verification take?
After submission, institute verification takes 1-2 weeks. District-level verification takes another 2-4 weeks. Scholarship amounts are disbursed in 1-2 installments per academic year.

articles.title: Passport Application in Bhopal – How to Book Appointment at PSK & Get Passport Fast
articles.description: Complete guide to apply for a passport in Bhopal. Book appointment at Bhopal Passport Seva Kendra, document checklist, fees, and tracking.

content.text: Planning to apply for a passport in Bhopal? The process is now mostly online, and if you follow the right steps, you can get your passport in 7-10 working days. Here is the complete guide for 2026.
Bhopal Passport Seva Kendra (PSK)
Bhopal has one Passport Seva Kendra (PSK) that handles all passport applications:

items: Location: Passport Seva Kendra, 1st Floor, A-Wing, Paryavaran Parisar, E-5, Arera Colony, Bhopal - 462016
Working Hours: Monday to Friday, 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM
Phone: 1800-258-1800 (toll-free)

content.text: Step 1: Online Application

content.title: Visit Passport Seva Website
content.text: Go to https://portal2.passportindia.gov.in and register with your email and mobile number.

content.title: Fill the Application Form
content.text: Select 'Apply for Fresh Passport/Re-issue'. Enter all details carefully — name, DOB, address must match your supporting documents exactly.

content.title: Pay the Fee
content.text: Fees are paid online after form submission. Current fee structure:

content.title: Book Appointment at Bhopal PSK
content.text: After payment, the portal shows available appointment slots at Bhopal PSK. Choose a date and time. Tatkaal appointments are available separately but have limited slots.
Normal appointments are available 2-4 weeks ahead. Tatkaal (expedited) appointments can be booked 2-5 days ahead but are more expensive. The Tatkaal quota opens at 9:00 AM daily — book early.
Step 2: Visit PSK with Documents
Required documents checklist:

items: Printed application form (with barcode)
Original Aadhaar + photocopy (Proof of Address + ID)
Original date of birth proof (Class 10 marksheet / birth certificate / Aadhaar)
Address proof (Aadhaar / Voter ID / electricity bill / rent agreement)
Passport-size photos (white background, 2"x2") — photos are taken at PSK anyway
Existing passport (if re-issue)
Marriage certificate (if name changed after marriage)

content.title: At the PSK
content.text: Reach 30 minutes before your slot. Process: document verification → photographs → biometrics (fingerprints) → police verification is initiated automatically. The entire process takes 2-3 hours at the PSK.
Step 3: Police Verification

After your PSK visit, local police will visit your residence for verification. In Bhopal, this is usually completed within 5-7 days. Ensure someone is home during working hours. Carry the acknowledgement receipt.
If you submit incorrect address details, police verification will fail and your application will be rejected. Double-check all address details before applying.
Step 4: Passport Dispatch

Once police verification is cleared (usually 3-5 working days after police report submission), your passport is printed and dispatched via Speed Post. Track your application on the Passport Seva portal using your ARN (Application Reference Number).
Bhopal Passport Timelines

articles.title: How to Link Aadhaar with PAN Online – Step by Step Guide 2026
articles.description: Link Aadhaar with PAN card online in 2 minutes. Follow this simple guide to link on Income Tax e-filing portal. Check status & avoid penalty.

content.text: Linking your Aadhaar with PAN is mandatory for filing income tax returns and for PAN to remain valid. The process takes just 2 minutes online. Here is exactly how to do it.
Why Link Aadhaar with PAN?

As per the Income Tax Act, linking Aadhaar with PAN is mandatory. If you don't link, your PAN becomes inoperative, meaning you cannot file returns, make high-value transactions, or use PAN for financial purposes.
Method: Link on Income Tax e-Filing Portal

content.title: Visit Income Tax e-Filing Portal
content.text: Go to https://www.incometax.gov.in and log in with your PAN and password. New users can register with PAN and Aadhaar.

content.title: Go to 'Link Aadhaar' Section
content.text: On the dashboard, click on 'Profile Settings' → 'Link Aadhaar'. Alternatively, use the quick link on the homepage.

content.title: Enter Aadhaar Number
content.text: Enter your 12-digit Aadhaar number. Your name, DOB, and gender will auto-populate from PAN database. Verify they match your Aadhaar.

content.title: Click 'Link Aadhaar'
content.text: Click the button. An OTP will be sent to your Aadhaar-registered mobile. Enter the OTP to complete linking.
Use an OTP of ₹10 is charged for this service (via the e-filing portal). SMS charges apply based on your operator. No GST or extra fees.
Alternative: Link via SMS or NSDL
You can also link by sending an SMS to 567678 or 56161 in the format: UIDPAN <12 digit Aadhaar> <10 digit PAN>. Or use the NSDL portal at https://www.tin-nsdl.com/services/pan/link-aadhaar.html.
How to Check if Aadhaar is Already Linked to PAN

content.title: Visit Income Tax Portal
content.text: Go to the e-filing portal and navigate to 'Link Aadhaar Status'. Enter your PAN and Aadhaar to check if already linked.

content.title: Alternative: Use Quick Link
content.text: On the Income Tax homepage, use the 'Link Aadhaar Status' quick link. Enter PAN only to see linking status.
Common Issues & Fixes

rows: Update name on Aadhaar (online at UIDAI) or PAN (via NSDL correction form)
Update DOB on PAN via NSDL correction with proof (birth certificate, school marksheet)
Visit nearest Aadhaar enrollment center to update mobile number
Wait 5 minutes and try again. If persistent, use SMS method instead

content.text: Penalty for Not Linking

If PAN is not linked with Aadhaar, the PAN becomes 'inoperative'. You won't be able to file IT returns, pay taxes online using PAN, or make high-value transactions. There's a late fee of ₹1,000 if linking is done after the deadline.

articles.title: How to Upload Documents in DigiLocker – Step by Step Guide
articles.description: Learn how to upload your own documents (self-uploaded) in DigiLocker. Complete guide with screenshots and tips for Bharat students.

content.text: While government-issued documents in DigiLocker are the most valuable, you can also upload your own documents (self-uploaded). This guide shows you how to do it step by step.
What You Can Upload
You can store digital copies of any document in your DigiLocker. Useful for:

items: College ID cards
Previous marksheets not in the Issued Documents system
Experience certificates
Achievement certificates
Medical reports and prescriptions
Property documents (sale deed, registry)

content.text: Step-by-Step Process

content.title: Open DigiLocker
content.text: Open the DigiLocker app or go to https://digilocker.gov.in and log in with your Aadhaar and PIN.

content.title: Go to 'Uploaded Documents'
content.text: In the dashboard, select 'Uploaded Documents' section (different from Issued Documents). This is where your self-uploaded files live.

content.title: Click '+' or 'Upload'
content.text: Click the upload button. Select the document category (Academic, Medical, Identity, etc.) or create a custom label.

content.title: Select File
content.text: Choose a PDF or image file from your phone/computer. Maximum file size is 10MB per document. Supported formats: PDF, JPG, PNG.

content.title: Add Document Name
content.text: Give your document a clear name (e.g., 'B.Tech Semester 4 Marksheet'). This makes finding it later easy.

content.title: Submit
content.text: Click upload. Your document is now stored in DigiLocker and accessible from any device.

Self-uploaded documents are not 'verified' by any authority. For official verification, use the 'Issued Documents' section. However, many colleges and employers accept self-uploaded DigiLocker documents for internal purposes.

articles.title: How to Update Mobile Number in Aadhaar Online 2026
articles.description: Update your mobile number in Aadhaar online. Step-by-step guide for UIDAI portal, mAadhaar app, and nearest enrollment center. Free process in 2026.

content.text: Your Aadhaar-registered mobile number is essential for receiving OTPs, checking status, and using online services. If your number has changed, here is exactly how to update it in 2026.
Why Update Mobile Number in Aadhaar?
An updated mobile number lets you: download e-Aadhaar anytime, receive OTP for online verification, check Aadhaar status via SMS, use mAadhaar app, and link Aadhaar with PAN and bank accounts.
Method 1: Visit Aadhaar Enrollment Center (Recommended)

content.title: Find Nearest Center
content.text: Visit https://appointments.uidai.gov.in to find your nearest Aadhaar enrollment center. Enter your PIN code and select 'Book Appointment'.

content.title: Fill Aadhaar Correction Form
content.text: Download Form Aadhaar Correction from UIDAI website. Fill in your details: name, Aadhaar number, old and new mobile number.

content.title: Visit with Documents
content.text: Take the filled form, your Aadhaar card copy, and any identity proof to the center. The process takes 10-15 minutes. Biometric verification is required.
This method is free. The mobile number is updated immediately in the UIDAI database after biometric verification. You'll receive a confirmation SMS on the new number.
Method 2: Self-Service Update Portal

Visit https://ssup.uidai.gov.in. Enter your Aadhaar number and the OTP sent to your registered mobile (if you still have access to the old number). You can request a mobile update, but final verification still requires the enrollment center visit.
Documents Required

items: Aadhaar card (original or copy)
Identity proof (Aadhaar itself is usually sufficient)
Correction form (available at center)

content.text: What If You Don't Have Access to Old Mobile?
If your old number is no longer active, you must visit the enrollment center with biometric verification. There is no online-only method to change mobile number without access to the old number.

articles.title: How to Download Aadhaar Card Online – e-Aadhaar Download 2026
articles.description: Download e-Aadhaar online from UIDAI website. Free, instant download using Aadhaar number, Enrolment ID, or Virtual ID. Step-by-step guide.

content.text: You don't need to wait for the physical Aadhaar letter to arrive by post. You can download e-Aadhaar (digital copy) instantly from the UIDAI website. Here's how.
What is e-Aadhaar?

e-Aadhaar is a digitally signed PDF copy of your Aadhaar card. It is password-protected and legally valid everywhere the physical card is accepted. The password format is: first 4 letters of your name (as on Aadhaar) + year of birth (YYYY).
Method 1: Download by Aadhaar Number

content.title: Visit UIDAI Website
content.text: Go to https://eaadhaar.uidai.gov.in. This is the official e-Aadhaar download portal.

content.title: Select 'Download Aadhaar'
content.text: Click on 'Download Aadhaar' option. Select 'Aadhaar Number' as the input method.

content.title: Enter Details
content.text: Enter your 12-digit Aadhaar number, full name (as on Aadhaar), and PIN code. Complete the captcha.

content.title: Request OTP
content.text: Click 'Send OTP'. An OTP will be sent to your Aadhaar-registered mobile number.

content.title: Download e-Aadhaar
content.text: Enter the OTP and click 'Download'. A password-protected PDF will be downloaded. Use the password format: first 4 letters of name (CAPITAL) + YYYY birth year.
Download e-Aadhaar on multiple devices and keep a backup in cloud storage (Google Drive, iCloud). The password-protected PDF is safe to store digitally.
Method 2: Download by Enrollment ID
If you haven't received your Aadhaar yet, use your 14-digit Enrollment ID from the acknowledgement slip. The process is the same as above — select 'Enrolment ID' instead of 'Aadhaar Number'.
What If Your Mobile is Not Registered?

If your mobile is not linked to Aadhaar, you cannot download e-Aadhaar online. Visit the nearest Aadhaar enrollment center to update your mobile number first (see our guide: 'How to Update Mobile Number in Aadhaar').

articles.title: How to Check PAN Card Status Online 2026 – NSDL & UTIITSL Tracking
articles.description: Track your PAN card application status online via NSDL and UTIITSL portals. Check if PAN is allotted, dispatched, or pending. Instant tracking guide.

content.text: Applied for a new PAN card or requested a correction? Here is how to check the status of your application on NSDL and UTIITSL portals.
Method 1: NSDL PAN Status Check

content.title: Visit NSDL Tracking Page
content.text: Go to https://tin.tin.nsdl.com/pan/StatusTrack.html. This is the official NSDL PAN tracking page.

content.title: Enter Application Number
content.text: Enter your 15-digit acknowledgement number. You received this when you submitted your PAN application.

content.title: Enter Captcha
content.text: Type the captcha code and click 'Submit'. Your current status is displayed.
Method 2: UTIITSL PAN Status Check

content.title: Visit UTIITSL
content.text: Go to https://www.utiitsl.com and select 'PAN Services' → 'Track PAN Application'.

content.title: Enter Details
content.text: Enter your acknowledgement number and registered mobile number. You'll receive an OTP for verification.

content.title: View Status
content.text: After OTP verification, your complete application status with tracking details is displayed.
Understanding PAN Application Status

rows: Application is being verified by NSDL/UTIITSL
Physical card sent via speed post
Application had errors or incomplete documents

content.text: Once status shows 'Allotted', you can immediately download e-PAN from NSDL/UTIITSL websites. You don't need to wait for the physical card.

articles.title: Driving License Test Tips for Bhopal RTO – H& Track Test Guide 2026
articles.description: Pass your driving license test at Bhopal RTO. Complete guide for H-track, road test, reverse, and hill start. Tips, videos, and common mistakes.

content.text: The driving test at Bhopal RTO can be nerve-wracking, but with the right preparation, you can pass it on your first attempt. This guide covers everything you need to know about the H-track test, road test, and common mistakes to avoid.
Types of Driving Tests
At Bhopal RTO, the driving test has two parts:

items: H-Track (Ground Test) — For two-wheelers and four-wheelers. Tests your control in a marked H-shaped track.
Road Test — For four-wheelers (LMV). Tests your actual driving on city roads near the RTO.

content.text: H-Track Test: What to Expect
The H-track is a rectangular space marked with cones. You must drive through it without touching any cones. Key elements:

items: Enter the track from the entry point
Drive in reverse around the H-shaped path
Exit without touching cones or stopping the engine
The entire test is observed by an RTO inspector

content.text: Tips for H-Track Test

items: Practice in an open ground before the test. Mark cones with water bottles.
Use your side mirrors constantly — they help you judge cone distance.
Move slowly — clutch control is key. The slower you go, the more time you have to adjust.
If you're about to hit a cone, stop and re-adjust instead of rushing.
For two-wheelers, keep your feet on the foot pegs (not on the ground) once you enter the track.
For cars, use the rearview and side mirrors to steer in reverse.

content.text: Road Test (LMV) Tips
For four-wheelers, the road test is conducted on actual roads near Bhopal RTO. The inspector sits beside you and evaluates:

items: Seatbelt check — MUST wear before starting the engine
Mirror adjustment — adjust mirrors before starting
Signal usage — use indicator for all turns and lane changes
Traffic rules — obey traffic lights, stop at zebra crossing, give way to pedestrians
Speed control — maintain steady speed; don't drive too fast or too slow
Clutch and gear control — smooth gear changes, no jerky movements

content.title: Before Starting the Engine
content.text: Fasten seatbelt → Adjust seat → Adjust mirrors → Check gear is in neutral → Start engine. The inspector notices all these steps.

content.title: During the Drive
content.text: Always use indicators for turns. Check mirrors every 10-15 seconds. Maintain safe following distance. Drive at 20-30 km/h on RTO roads.

content.title: Common Mistakes That Fail
content.text: Stopping the engine mid-test, touching cones in H-track, not using indicators, crossing the center line, not stopping at stop signs, aggressive driving.
Arrive at Bhopal RTO at least 1 hour before your scheduled slot. Park your vehicle, observe other test-takers, and practice mentally. Bring your own vehicle — you'll be comfortable with its controls.

articles.title: Passport Renewal in Bhopal – Online Re-issue Process 2026
articles.description: Renew your passport online from Bhopal. Complete guide for passport re-issue, appointment at Bhopal PSK, documents needed, and fees. Tatkaal option available.

content.text: Is your passport expired or about to expire? You can renew it easily online. Here is the complete passport renewal (re-issue) process for Bhopal residents.
When to Renew Your Passport

You should apply for passport re-issue in these cases: passport expired, passport expiring within 1 year, no blank pages left for visas, change in personal details (name, address, appearance), damaged passport, or lost passport.
Step-by-Step Renewal Process

content.title: Visit Passport Seva Portal
content.text: Go to https://portal2.passportindia.gov.in and log in with your registered account. If you don't have an account, create one with your email and mobile.

content.title: Select 'Re-issue of Passport'
content.text: Choose 'Re-issue of Passport' as the application type. Select the reason that applies to you (expired, expiring, change in appearance, etc.).

content.title: Fill the Application
content.text: The form auto-fills many details from your previous passport. Verify and update any changed information, especially address, phone number, and emergency contact.

content.title: Pay the Fee
content.text: Fees are paid online. Standard re-issue fee (36-page): ₹1,500. Jumbo (60-page): ₹2,000. Tatkaal: add ₹1,000 extra.

content.title: Book Appointment at Bhopal PSK
content.text: Select Bhopal Passport Seva Kendra as your PSK. Choose an available date and time. Normal appointments are available 2-3 weeks ahead.

content.title: Visit PSK with Old Passport
content.text: On the appointment day, carry your old passport, print of the application form, and address proof (if address has changed). Your old passport will be cancelled and returned with a hole punch.
Documents for Renewal

items: Old passport (original + first/last page copy)
Passport application form (printed with barcode)
Address proof (only if address changed from previous passport)
Passport-size photos (white background)

content.text: If your address hasn't changed from your previous passport, you don't need any address proof for renewal. The old passport serves as both identity and address proof.

articles.title: How to Verify Documents on DigiLocker – QR Code & URI Verification Guide
articles.description: Verify digital documents from DigiLocker using QR code scan or URI link. Accepted by colleges, employers, and government offices across India.

content.text: One of the best features of DigiLocker is document verification. Employers, colleges, and government offices can instantly verify your documents without seeing the original. Here is how verification works.
What Makes DigiLocker Documents Verifiable?

Documents issued through DigiLocker's 'Issued Documents' section are digitally signed by the issuing authority (CBSE, MP Board, etc.). Each document has a unique QR code and a URI (Uniform Resource Identifier) that points to the verified copy on DigiLocker servers.
Two Ways to Share Verified Documents
1. QR Code (Digital or Printed)
Each document in DigiLocker has a QR code at the top. You can:

items: Show the QR code on your phone screen — verifier scans it with any QR scanner
Print the document — the QR code on the printout can still be scanned
The QR code opens a secure verification page on DigiLocker showing the original document

content.text: 2. Share URI Link
Each document has a unique URI (a long URL). You can:

items: Click 'Share' and send the URI via email or WhatsApp
The verifier opens the link in any browser — it shows the official verified copy
No login required for the verifier. The link works for 30 days.

content.text: What Verifiers See

When someone scans your QR code or opens the URI, they see: the exact document as issued by the authority, document issue date and issuer details, digital signature verification status (✓ Verified), and tamper detection (shows if document was modified).

DigiLocker documents are recognized as legally valid under the IT Act, 2000. Most Indian universities, government exams (SSC, UPSC), and private employers accept DigiLocker verified documents without needing physical copies.
